title: BHIV Capability Registry Schema
description: Schema for a single capability record in the BHIV capability registry. A capability is a reusable, scope-bounded function with declared inputs, outputs, and authority limits. It is not a product, team, or authority.

capability_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this capability. Format: UUID-5 derived from capability_name + owner + version. Immutable after ACTIVE status.

capability_name.description: Human-readable name for the capability. Must be unique within the registry. Use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

owner.description: Declares who owns this capability. Ownership is a declared structural field, not an authority grant.

contact.description: Contact reference (email, Slack channel, or on-call alias).

version.description: Semantic version of this capability record. Incrementing produces a new record; old record moves to DEPRECATED.

scope.description: Availability boundary of this capability across the BHIV ecosystem.

dependencies.description: List of other capability_ids that this capability requires to function. Empty array means no dependencies.

attachment_rules.description: Defines how a consumer may attach to this capability. See ATTACHMENT_MODEL.md for full attachment type definitions.

attachment_type.description: How the capability is integrated into a consumer system.

endpoint.description: Optional. Entry point for api_linked or sidecar attachment types.

idempotent.description: Whether repeated invocations with identical inputs produce identical outputs.

authority_limits.description: Declares what this capability owns and does not own. Mirrors the AuthorityDeclaration pattern from governance_authority.py. The capability itself is not an authority — authority_limits describes the boundary.

owns.description: Explicit list of responsibilities this capability controls.

does_not_own.description: Explicit list of responsibilities this capability delegates or explicitly excludes.

requires_governance_approval.description: Whether attaching this capability requires approval from a governance authority.

inputs.description: Declared input parameters this capability accepts.

outputs.description: Declared output fields this capability produces.

consumers.description: Known systems or products that consume this capability. Informational — not an enforcement list.

consumer_id.description: Unique identifier for the consuming system.

consumer_name.description: Human-readable name of the consuming system.

documentation_reference.description: Pointers to authoritative documentation for this capability.

primary.description: Path or URL to the primary documentation file.

schema_file.description: Optional. Path to a JSON Schema or contract definition file.

source_file.description: Optional. Path to the primary source file implementing this capability.

inputs.name: artifact_id
inputs.description: Unique ID of the artifact to check

outputs.name: sequence_id
outputs.description: Monotonic sequence number assigned on ACCEPTED
